mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and housing starts signal broad-based economic expansion, which lifts aggregate demand expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing, or forward returns turn negative when both INDPRO and HOUST rise concurrently for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with accelerating core inflation compress equity risk premiums as the Federal Reserve signals aggressive tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.10pp in 3 months AND CPIAUCSL rises > 0.20pp in 3 months
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in over 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP expands the corporate earnings base, which mechanically supports higher equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a six-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDPC1 grows quarter-over-quarter, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 new jobs in a single month confirms robust labor demand, which boosts consumer confidence and equity risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of qualifying months, or the average 5-day return following strong payroll prints is not statistically greater than the average return following weak prints (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances alongside falling unemployment indicate accelerating household spending power, which drives revenue growth expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict returns better than a single-proxy baseline, or forward returns turn negative when RSAFS rises and UNRATE falls simultaneously for two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production sustaining above 100 while inflation remains below 2.5 percent signals a benign supply-demand balance that supports corporate margins and equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are negative in more than 55% of instances meeting the threshold, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when supply chain disruptions cause INDPRO volatility to exceed 1.5 standard deviations.
